facts.text: Spring Hill’s name first appeared on 1856 maps of Hernando County, likely pointing to local freshwater springs amid oak forests.
The community began modern development in 1967 when the Mackle brothers’ Deltona Corporation launched a master‑planned retirement enclave.
Spring Hill covers roughly 60 square miles of land, offering a mix of single‑family homes, condos, and commercial areas along U.S. routes.
Spring Hill’s early settlers were drawn by the region’s natural springs and scattered lakes, shaping its rural character before the 1960s.
The Mackle brothers’ Deltona Corporation, founded by Frank, Elliott, and Robert Mackle, envisioned Spring Hill as a quiet alternative to urban Florida living.
